target: Espero que estés bien.
english: I hope you're well.
grammar: "Espero que" + subjunctive — expressing hope.

target: Quiero que vengas a la fiesta.
english: I want you to come to the party.

target: Es necesario que todos asistan.
english: It's necessary that everyone attend.

target: Busco un lugar donde pueda estudiar.
english: I'm looking for a place where I can study.
grammar: Subjunctive after indefinite antecedent.

target: Te llamo para que sepas.
english: I'm calling so that you know.
grammar: "Para que" + subjunctive — purpose.

target: Antes de que te vayas, escúchame.
english: Before you leave, listen to me.

target: Necesito que me ayudes.
english: I need you to help me.

target: Con tal de que llegues a tiempo.
english: As long as you arrive on time.

target: Es poco probable que ganemos.
english: It's unlikely that we'll win.

target: Me sorprende que hable tan bien.
english: It surprises me that he speaks so well.

target: Es mejor que lleguemos temprano.
english: It's better that we arrive early.

target: Espero que el vuelo no se retrase.
english: I hope the flight isn't delayed.

target: Es fundamental que los empleados estén motivados.
english: It's fundamental that employees be motivated.

target: Me alegra que mis hijos sean responsables.
english: I'm glad my children are responsible.

target: Ven aquí, por favor.
english: Come here, please.
grammar: Informal affirmative command of "venir."

target: No hables tan alto.
english: Don't speak so loud.
grammar: Negative informal command — uses subjunctive.

target: Dime la verdad.
english: Tell me the truth.

target: Siéntate aquí.
english: Sit down here.

target: No te preocupes.
english: Don't worry.

target: Abre la ventana.
english: Open the window.

target: Cierra la puerta.
english: Close the door.

target: Escúchame bien.
english: Listen to me carefully.

target: Siga todo recto.
english: Go straight ahead.
grammar: Formal command of "seguir."

target: Gire a la derecha.
english: Turn right.

target: No toque eso.
english: Don't touch that.
grammar: Formal negative command.

target: Póngase el cinturón.
english: Put on your seatbelt.

target: Escriba su nombre aquí.
english: Write your name here.

target: No se olvide de firmar.
english: Don't forget to sign.

target: Pasa y siéntate.
english: Come in and sit down.

target: Toma, esto es para ti.
english: Here, this is for you.

target: Sal de aquí ahora mismo.
english: Get out of here right now.

target: Pon la mesa, por favor.
english: Set the table, please.

target: Haz tu tarea antes de jugar.
english: Do your homework before playing.

target: Ten cuidado con el tráfico.
english: Be careful with the traffic.

target: Vete a dormir, es tarde.
english: Go to sleep, it's late.

target: No llegues tarde mañana.
english: Don't be late tomorrow.

target: Come más despacio.
english: Eat more slowly.

target: Cállate un momento.
english: Be quiet for a moment.

target: Llámame cuando llegues.
english: Call me when you arrive.

target: Me gustaría un café.
english: I would like a coffee.
grammar: Conditional for polite requests.

target: ¿Podrías ayudarme?
english: Could you help me?

target: Sería bueno descansar un poco.
english: It would be good to rest a little.

target: ¿Te importaría cerrar la ventana?
english: Would you mind closing the window?

target: Yo en tu lugar, no haría eso.
english: In your place, I wouldn't do that.

target: Deberías estudiar más.
english: You should study more.

target: ¿Qué harías con un millón de euros?
english: What would you do with a million euros?

target: Me encantaría viajar a Japón.
english: I'd love to travel to Japan.

target: Podríamos ir al cine esta noche.
english: We could go to the cinema tonight.

target: ¿Te gustaría cenar conmigo?
english: Would you like to have dinner with me?

target: Haría cualquier cosa por mi familia.
english: I would do anything for my family.

target: Tendría que pensarlo.
english: I would have to think about it.

target: ¿Podrían darme más información?
english: Could you give me more information?

target: Diría que tiene razón.
english: I would say he's right.

target: No sabría qué decir.
english: I wouldn't know what to say.

target: ¿Querrías trabajar con nosotros?
english: Would you want to work with us?

target: Saldría más temprano si pudiera.
english: I'd leave earlier if I could.

target: Nos vendría bien un descanso.
english: A break would do us good.

target: Me iría de vacaciones ahora mismo.
english: I'd go on vacation right now.

target: Eso costaría demasiado.
english: That would cost too much.

target: ¿Cuánto tardaría en llegar?
english: How long would it take to arrive?

target: Convendría revisar el contrato.
english: It would be advisable to review the contract.

target: Valdría la pena intentarlo.
english: It would be worth trying.

target: Preferería quedarme en casa.
english: I'd prefer to stay home.

target: ¿Dónde te gustaría vivir?
english: Where would you like to live?

target: Mañana iré al médico.
english: Tomorrow I'll go to the doctor.

target: ¿Vendrás a la fiesta?
english: Will you come to the party?

target: El año que viene estudiaré francés.
english: Next year I'll study French.

target: Cuando sea mayor, seré bombero.
english: When I grow up, I'll be a firefighter.

target: He comido demasiado.
english: I've eaten too much.
grammar: Present perfect: haber + past participle.

target: Nunca he visto nada igual.
english: I've never seen anything like it.

target: Ya hemos terminado.
english: We've already finished.

target: Todavía no ha llegado.
english: He still hasn't arrived.

target: Nunca había comido paella antes.
english: I had never eaten paella before.

target: Ya habíamos reservado el hotel.
english: We had already booked the hotel.

target: Para las seis, habré terminado.
english: By six, I will have finished.
grammar: Future perfect: habré + past participle.

target: Llevo tres años estudiando español.
english: I've been studying Spanish for three years.
grammar: "Llevar" + time + gerund — ongoing duration.

target: Llevo una hora esperando.
english: I've been waiting for an hour.

target: ¿Cuánto tiempo llevas viviendo aquí?
english: How long have you been living here?

target: Acabo de llegar.
english: I just arrived.
grammar: "Acabar de" + infinitive — to have just done.

target: Acaba de llamar tu madre.
english: Your mother just called.

target: El tren está a punto de salir.
english: The train is about to leave.
grammar: to be about to.

target: Voy a empezar un curso nuevo.
english: I'm going to start a new course.

target: ¿Vas a venir o no?
english: Are you going to come or not?

target: Seguiré intentándolo.
english: I'll keep trying.

target: Habrá que esperar.
english: We'll have to wait.

target: ¿Cuándo volverás?
english: When will you return?

target: La persona que llamó no dejó su nombre.
english: The person who called didn't leave their name.

target: El libro que estoy leyendo es fascinante.
english: The book I'm reading is fascinating.

target: La ciudad donde nací ya no es la misma.
english: The city where I was born is no longer the same.

target: Todo lo que dijo era mentira.
english: Everything he said was a lie.

target: La razón por la que vinimos es importante.
english: The reason we came is important.

target: El restaurante en el que cenamos era excelente.
english: The restaurant where we had dinner was excellent.

target: La empresa para la que trabajo es internacional.
english: The company I work for is international.

target: El amigo con quien viajé es muy divertido.
english: The friend I traveled with is very fun.

target: La casa en la que crecí era pequeña.
english: The house I grew up in was small.

target: Lo que más me gusta es la música.
english: What I like most is music.

target: El profesor, cuya clase es difícil, es muy bueno.
english: The teacher, whose class is hard, is very good.
grammar: whose (relative possessive).

target: Esa es la chica con la que hablé.
english: That's the girl I talked to.

target: No entiendo lo que quieres decir.
english: I don't understand what you mean.

target: El día en que nos conocimos fue especial.
english: The day we met was special.

target: La manera en que habla es muy clara.
english: The way he speaks is very clear.

target: Hay algo que quiero contarte.
english: There's something I want to tell you.

target: El hotel donde nos quedamos tenía piscina.
english: The hotel where we stayed had a pool.

target: La persona a quien busco no está aquí.
english: The person I'm looking for isn't here.

target: Lo que pasó ayer fue increíble.
english: What happened yesterday was incredible.

target: El momento en el que todo cambió.
english: The moment when everything changed.

target: El país del que vengo es muy bonito.
english: The country I come from is very beautiful.

target: Las personas con las que trabajo son geniales.
english: The people I work with are great.

target: El problema al que nos enfrentamos es serio.
english: The problem we face is serious.

target: Eso es exactamente lo que necesitaba.
english: That's exactly what I needed.

target: La mujer cuyo hijo conociste es mi vecina.
english: The woman whose son you met is my neighbor.

target: Si tuviera más tiempo, viajaría más.
english: If I had more time, I'd travel more.
grammar: Imperfect subjunctive + conditional — second conditional.

target: Quería que vinieras a mi boda.
english: I wanted you to come to my wedding.

target: Si pudiera elegir, viviría en la costa.
english: If I could choose, I'd live on the coast.

target: Me pidieron que llegara temprano.
english: They asked me to arrive early.

target: Ojalá tuviera más dinero.
english: I wish I had more money.

target: Si fuera tú, no haría eso.
english: If I were you, I wouldn't do that.

target: Era necesario que todos participaran.
english: It was necessary that everyone participate.

target: Si supiera la respuesta, te la diría.
english: If I knew the answer, I'd tell you.

target: Me gustaría que estuvieras aquí.
english: I'd like you to be here.

target: Si hablara español mejor, conseguiría ese trabajo.
english: If I spoke Spanish better, I'd get that job.

target: Dudaba que viniera.
english: I doubted he would come.

target: Me sorprendió que no dijera nada.
english: It surprised me that she didn't say anything.

target: Si viviera en España, comería tapas todos los días.
english: If I lived in Spain, I'd eat tapas every day.

target: El jefe quería que termináramos antes.
english: The boss wanted us to finish earlier.

target: Si no lloviera, iríamos a la playa.
english: If it weren't raining, we'd go to the beach.

target: No había nadie que supiera la respuesta.
english: There was nobody who knew the answer.

target: Si pudieras cambiar algo, ¿qué sería?
english: If you could change something, what would it be?

target: Buscaba un hotel que tuviera piscina.
english: I was looking for a hotel that had a pool.

target: Si mi abuela estuviera aquí, estaría orgullosa.
english: If my grandmother were here, she'd be proud.

target: Esperaba que me llamaras.
english: I was hoping you'd call me.

target: Si ganara la lotería, dejaría de trabajar.
english: If I won the lottery, I'd stop working.

target: Era importante que practicáramos todos los días.
english: It was important that we practice every day.

target: Si tuviera carro, te llevaría.
english: If I had a car, I'd give you a ride.

target: No creía que fuera posible.
english: I didn't believe it was possible.

target: Si estuviera en tu lugar, aceptaría la oferta.
english: If I were in your place, I'd accept the offer.

target: Si hubiera sabido, habría venido antes.
english: If I had known, I would have come earlier.
grammar: Third conditional: pluperfect subjunctive + conditional perfect.

target: Habría ido si me hubieras invitado.
english: I would have gone if you had invited me.

target: Si hubiéramos salido antes, no habríamos llegado tarde.
english: If we had left earlier, we wouldn't have arrived late.

target: De haber sabido la verdad, habría actuado diferente.
english: Had I known the truth, I would have acted differently.
grammar: "De haber" + participle — literary third conditional.

target: ¿Habrías hecho lo mismo en mi lugar?
english: Would you have done the same in my place?

target: Si no hubiera llovido, habríamos ido al parque.
english: If it hadn't rained, we would have gone to the park.

target: Habríamos ganado si hubiéramos jugado mejor.
english: We would have won if we had played better.

target: Si hubiera estudiado medicina, ahora sería doctor.
english: If I had studied medicine, I'd be a doctor now.
grammar: Mixed conditional: past condition, present result.

target: No habría pasado si hubieras tenido más cuidado.
english: It wouldn't have happened if you had been more careful.

target: Si hubiera aceptado ese trabajo, viviría en Madrid.
english: If I had accepted that job, I'd be living in Madrid.

target: Habría sido mejor que esperáramos.
english: It would have been better if we had waited.

target: Si nos hubiéramos conocido antes, todo sería diferente.
english: If we had met earlier, everything would be different.

target: Si no hubiera llovido, habríamos hecho la excursión.
english: If it hadn't rained, we would have done the excursion.

target: Habría preferido que me lo dijeras.
english: I would have preferred that you told me.

target: Si hubiera nacido en otro país, hablaría otro idioma.
english: If I had been born in another country, I'd speak another language.

target: Si me hubieras escuchado, no tendríamos este problema.
english: If you had listened to me, we wouldn't have this problem.

target: Habría sido más fácil con tu ayuda.
english: It would have been easier with your help.

target: Si hubiera tenido más experiencia, me habrían contratado.
english: If I had had more experience, they would have hired me.

target: Si lo hubiera sabido, no habría dicho nada.
english: If I had known, I wouldn't have said anything.

target: De haberlo visto, te habría avisado.
english: Had I seen it, I would have warned you.

target: Habría venido antes de haber podido.
english: I would have come earlier if I could have.

target: Si hubieran llegado a tiempo, habrían visto el espectáculo.
english: If they had arrived on time, they would have seen the show.

target: No habría funcionado aunque lo hubiéramos intentado.
english: It wouldn't have worked even if we had tried.

target: Si hubieras venido, te habrías divertido.
english: If you had come, you would have had fun.

target: Se habla español en veinte países.
english: Spanish is spoken in twenty countries.
grammar: "Se" + verb — passive/impersonal construction.

target: Se necesitan voluntarios.
english: Volunteers are needed.

target: Aquí se come muy bien.
english: The food here is very good.

target: Se prohíbe fumar.
english: Smoking is prohibited.

target: Se dice que va a llover.
english: They say it's going to rain.

target: Se venden departamentos baratos.
english: Cheap apartments for sale.

target: Se buscan programadores.
english: Programmers wanted.

target: Aquí se trabaja mucho.
english: People work a lot here.

target: El edificio fue construido en 1900.
english: The building was built in 1900.
grammar: Passive with "ser" + past participle.

target: La carta fue escrita por mi abuelo.
english: The letter was written by my grandfather.

target: Las ventanas fueron rotas por la tormenta.
english: The windows were broken by the storm.

target: El proyecto será presentado mañana.
english: The project will be presented tomorrow.

target: La decisión ya ha sido tomada.
english: The decision has already been made.

target: Se te cayó el vaso.
english: You dropped the glass.

target: Se me olvidaron las llaves.
english: I forgot my keys.

target: Hay que estudiar más.
english: One must study more.
grammar: "Hay que" — impersonal obligation.

target: No se puede estacionar aquí.
english: You can't park here.

target: Se recomienda llegar temprano.
english: It's recommended to arrive early.

target: Se ruega silencio.
english: Silence is requested.

target: El resultado fue anunciado por el director.
english: The result was announced by the director.

target: Dado que no tenemos opciones, aceptaremos.
english: Given that we have no options, we'll accept.

target: Si bien es cierto que cometí errores, aprendí.
english: While it's true I made mistakes, I learned.

target: A medida que pasa el tiempo, las cosas cambian.
english: As time passes, things change.

target: Siempre que tenga la oportunidad, viajo.
english: Whenever I have the opportunity, I travel.

target: A raíz de la pandemia, todo cambió.
english: As a result of the pandemic, everything changed.

target: No solo aprendió el idioma, sino que se sumergió en la cultura.
english: Not only did he learn the language, but he immersed himself in the culture.
grammar: not only...but.

target: Más allá de las diferencias, todos buscamos lo mismo.
english: Beyond our differences, we all seek the same thing.

target: A fin de que mejoren los resultados, debemos actuar.
english: In order for results to improve, we must act.

target: Sin que nadie se diera cuenta, se marchó.
english: Without anyone noticing, he left.

target: Por un lado, es buena idea; por otro, es arriesgada.
english: On one hand, it's a good idea; on the other, it's risky.

target: Puesto que ya es tarde, nos vamos.
english: Since it's already late, we're leaving.

target: Con respecto a tu pregunta, no tengo la respuesta.
english: Regarding your question, I don't have the answer.

target: A fin de cuentas, todos somos humanos.
english: At the end of the day, we're all human.

target: En lo que se refiere a la economía, hay optimismo.
english: As far as the economy is concerned, there's optimism.

target: Dondequiera que vayas, lleva un diccionario.
english: Wherever you go, carry a dictionary.

target: Haga lo que haga, nunca parece suficiente.
english: Whatever he does, it never seems enough.
grammar: Reduplicative subjunctive for emphasis.

target: La riqueza de un pueblo se mide por su cultura.
english: The wealth of a people is measured by its culture.

target: Cada lengua es una ventana a otra forma de ver el mundo.
english: Each language is a window to another way of seeing the world.

target: El arte tiene la capacidad de trascender las barreras.
english: Art has the ability to transcend barriers.

target: La empatía es la base de toda convivencia.
english: Empathy is the foundation of all coexistence.

target: El liderazgo efectivo requiere empatía y visión.
english: Effective leadership requires empathy and vision.

target: La tecnología ha revolucionado la comunicación.
english: Technology has revolutionized communication.

target: Siempre desayuno a las ocho.
english: I always have breakfast at eight.

target: Mi hermana trabaja en un hospital.
english: My sister works in a hospital.

target: Los lunes voy al gimnasio.
english: On Mondays I go to the gym.

target: Ellos viven cerca de la playa.
english: They live near the beach.

target: Nosotros hablamos español en casa.
english: We speak Spanish at home.

target: Mi padre cocina muy bien.
english: My father cooks very well.

target: Ella lee el periódico cada mañana.
english: She reads the newspaper every morning.

target: Yo corro en el parque por las tardes.
english: I run in the park in the afternoons.

target: Mis amigos juegan fútbol los sábados.
english: My friends play soccer on Saturdays.

target: Escribo correos electrónicos todo el día.
english: I write emails all day.

target: Mi jefe siempre llega tarde.
english: My boss always arrives late.

target: Comemos juntos los domingos.
english: We eat together on Sundays.

target: Ella estudia medicina en Madrid.
english: She studies medicine in Madrid.

target: Yo prefiero el café con leche.
english: I prefer coffee with milk.

target: Mi madre camina una hora cada día.
english: My mother walks one hour every day.

target: Ellos no entienden la pregunta.
english: They don't understand the question.

target: Nosotros cenamos a las nueve.
english: We have dinner at nine.

target: Él abre la tienda a las diez.
english: He opens the shop at ten.

target: Yo tomo el autobús para ir al trabajo.
english: I take the bus to go to work.

target: Mi vecino tiene tres perros.
english: My neighbor has three dogs.

target: Ella habla cuatro idiomas.
english: She speaks four languages.

target: Yo duermo solo seis horas.
english: I only sleep six hours.

target: Este restaurante cierra los martes.
english: This restaurant closes on Tuesdays.

target: Mi hijo aprende muy rápido.
english: My son learns very quickly.

target: Nosotros compartimos un departamento.
english: We share an apartment.

target: Ella sale del trabajo a las cinco.
english: She leaves work at five.

target: Yo compro fruta en el mercado.
english: I buy fruit at the market.

target: Mi abuela cuenta historias increíbles.
english: My grandmother tells incredible stories.

target: Él maneja demasiado rápido.
english: He drives too fast.

target: Nosotros practicamos yoga los miércoles.
english: We practice yoga on Wednesdays.

target: Ella conoce a todo el mundo aquí.
english: She knows everyone here.

target: Yo ahorro dinero cada mes.
english: I save money every month.

target: Los trenes salen cada media hora.
english: The trains leave every half hour.

target: Mi compañero de trabajo habla mucho.
english: My coworker talks a lot.

target: Ella pone música mientras cocina.
english: She plays music while she cooks.

target: Yo nunca bebo alcohol.
english: I never drink alcohol.

target: Mis hijos van al colegio en bicicleta.
english: My kids go to school by bicycle.

target: Él piensa demasiado las cosas.
english: He overthinks things.

target: La reunión empieza a las tres.
english: The meeting starts at three.
grammar: Present tense for scheduled events

target: Yo paso mucho tiempo en el teléfono.
english: I spend a lot of time on my phone.

target: Ella siempre dice la verdad.
english: She always tells the truth.

target: Mi perro come de todo.
english: My dog eats everything.

target: Nosotros pagamos el alquiler el día uno.
english: We pay the rent on the first.

target: Él parece cansado hoy.
english: He looks tired today.

target: Yo sé nadar pero no muy bien.
english: I know how to swim but not very well.

target: Mi familia vive en otra ciudad.
english: My family lives in another city.

target: Este café no tiene wifi.
english: This café doesn't have wifi.

target: Ella cree que es buena idea.
english: She thinks it's a good idea.

target: Los niños juegan en el patio.
english: The kids play in the yard.

target: Yo necesito unas vacaciones.
english: I need a vacation.

target: Mi teléfono suena todo el rato.
english: My phone rings all the time.

target: Ella quiere cambiar de trabajo.
english: She wants to change jobs.

target: Yo oigo ruidos por la noche.
english: I hear noises at night.

target: Nosotros hacemos ejercicio tres veces por semana.
english: We exercise three times a week.

target: Ella trae la comida de casa.
english: She brings food from home.

target: Mi primo vive en el extranjero.
english: My cousin lives abroad.

target: Yo siempre pierdo las llaves.
english: I always lose my keys.

target: El supermercado abre a las ocho.
english: The supermarket opens at eight.

target: Ella canta muy bien.
english: She sings very well.

target: Mi jefa sabe mucho de finanzas.
english: My boss knows a lot about finance.

target: Nosotros vemos una serie cada noche.
english: We watch a series every night.

target: Este barrio es muy tranquilo.
english: This neighborhood is very quiet.

target: Yo hago la cama todos los días.
english: I make the bed every day.

target: Ella pide café con hielo en verano.
english: She orders iced coffee in summer.

target: Mi hermano menor mide un metro ochenta.
english: My younger brother is one meter eighty tall.

target: Yo recuerdo tu nombre pero no tu apellido.
english: I remember your first name but not your last name.

target: Ella se preocupa demasiado por todo.
english: She worries too much about everything.

target: Yo envío muchos mensajes al día.
english: I send a lot of messages per day.

target: La farmacia está al lado del banco.
english: The pharmacy is next to the bank.

target: Nosotros quedamos los viernes para cenar.
english: We meet up on Fridays for dinner.

target: Ella trabaja desde casa los jueves.
english: She works from home on Thursdays.

target: Yo como demasiado rápido.
english: I eat too fast.

target: Mi cuñada es muy simpática.
english: My sister-in-law is very nice.

target: Él vende carros de segunda mano.
english: He sells second-hand cars.

target: Yo siempre olvido las contraseñas.
english: I always forget passwords.

target: Ella sonríe mucho.
english: She smiles a lot.

target: Mi mejor amigo vive en Barcelona.
english: My best friend lives in Barcelona.

target: Yo subo las escaleras en vez del ascensor.
english: I take the stairs instead of the elevator.

target: Los precios suben cada año.
english: Prices go up every year.

target: Ella lleva gafas.
english: She wears glasses.

target: Yo intento ser más paciente.
english: I try to be more patient.

target: Mi abuelo todavía trabaja.
english: My grandfather still works.

target: Este autobús para en todas las paradas.
english: This bus stops at every stop.

target: Ella nada en la piscina cada mañana.
english: She swims in the pool every morning.

target: Yo dejo propina siempre.
english: I always leave a tip.

target: Mi sobrino tiene cinco años.
english: My nephew is five years old.

target: Ella da clases de inglés.
english: She teaches English classes.

target: Yo me levanto a las seis y media.
english: I get up at six thirty.

target: El vuelo sale a medianoche.
english: The flight leaves at midnight.
grammar: Present tense for scheduled departures

target: Ella tiene una entrevista mañana.
english: She has an interview tomorrow.

target: Yo corto el pan con este cuchillo.
english: I cut the bread with this knife.

target: Nosotros reciclamos todo el plástico.
english: We recycle all the plastic.

target: Mi empresa tiene cien empleados.
english: My company has a hundred employees.

target: Ella merece un descanso.
english: She deserves a break.

target: Yo odio madrugar.
english: I hate getting up early.

target: Mi tía hace el mejor pastel de chocolate.
english: My aunt makes the best chocolate cake.

target: Él gana bastante dinero.
english: He earns quite a lot of money.

target: Yo creo que tiene razón.
english: I think she's right.

target: El museo abre de martes a domingo.
english: The museum is open Tuesday through Sunday.

target: Ella deja el carro en el estacionamiento.
english: She leaves the car in the parking lot.

target: Yo busco un regalo para mi madre.
english: I'm looking for a gift for my mother.

target: Mi novio cocina mejor que yo.
english: My boyfriend cooks better than me.

target: Nosotros comemos mucha fruta.
english: We eat a lot of fruit.

target: Ella enseña matemáticas en un instituto.
english: She teaches math at a high school.

target: Yo conozco un sitio barato para comer.
english: I know a cheap place to eat.

target: Mi vecina tiene un jardín precioso.
english: My neighbor has a beautiful garden.

target: Él ayuda a sus padres los fines de semana.
english: He helps his parents on weekends.

target: Yo entiendo más de lo que hablo.
english: I understand more than I speak.

target: La tienda de abajo vende de todo.
english: The shop downstairs sells everything.

target: Ella sigue una dieta sin gluten.
english: She follows a gluten-free diet.

target: Estoy cansado de esperar.
english: I'm tired of waiting.
grammar: Estar for temporary states

target: Mi padre es ingeniero.
english: My father is an engineer.
grammar: Ser for professions

target: La sopa está fría.
english: The soup is cold.
grammar: Estar for current condition

target: Somos cinco en mi familia.
english: There are five of us in my family.
grammar: Ser for inherent facts

target: El banco está cerrado hoy.
english: The bank is closed today.
grammar: Estar for resulting states

target: Ella es muy alta para su edad.
english: She's very tall for her age.
grammar: Ser for physical descriptions

target: Estamos en el aeropuerto.
english: We're at the airport.
grammar: Estar for location

target: Esta comida está deliciosa.
english: This food is delicious.
grammar: Estar for experience/taste

target: Mi hermano es el más joven.
english: My brother is the youngest.
grammar: Ser for identity

target: La oficina está en el tercer piso.
english: The office is on the third floor.
grammar: Estar for location

target: Él es de Colombia.
english: He is from Colombia.
grammar: Ser for origin

target: Estoy listo para salir.
english: I'm ready to go out.
grammar: Estar for temporary state

target: La fiesta es en mi casa.
english: The party is at my house.
grammar: Ser for events

target: Ella está enferma desde el lunes.
english: She's been sick since Monday.
grammar: Estar for health conditions

target: Este café es muy bueno.
english: This coffee is very good.
grammar: Ser for inherent quality

target: Estamos contentos con el resultado.
english: We're happy with the result.
grammar: Estar for emotional state

target: Mi mejor amiga es profesora.
english: My best friend is a teacher.
grammar: Ser for professions

target: El concierto es a las nueve.
english: The concert is at nine.
grammar: Ser for scheduled events

target: Estás muy guapa hoy.
english: You look really nice today.
grammar: Estar for temporary appearance

target: La llave está en el cajón.
english: The key is in the drawer.
grammar: Estar for location of objects

target: Él es bastante tímido.
english: He's quite shy.
grammar: Ser for personality traits

target: Estoy de acuerdo contigo.
english: I agree with you.
grammar: Estar in fixed expressions

target: La reunión es importante.
english: The meeting is important.
grammar: Ser for characteristics

target: Estamos perdidos.
english: We're lost.
grammar: Estar for temporary state

target: Ella es muy trabajadora.
english: She's very hardworking.
grammar: Ser for personality

target: El agua está muy caliente.
english: The water is very hot.
grammar: Estar for current temperature

target: Son las tres y media.
english: It's three thirty.
grammar: Ser for time

target: Estoy buscando un departamento nuevo.
english: I'm looking for a new apartment.
grammar: Estar + gerund for progressive

target: La película es en español.
english: The movie is in Spanish.
grammar: Ser for language of content

target: Estoy harto de este tráfico.
english: I'm fed up with this traffic.
grammar: Estar for emotional reactions

target: Mi casa es pequeña pero cómoda.

target: Ella siempre traía café para todos.
english: She always used to bring coffee for everyone.

target: De adolescente escuchaba mucho rock.
english: As a teenager, I listened to a lot of rock.

target: Antes podías estacionar gratis aquí.
english: Before, you could park here for free.

target: Mi tía vivía sola desde hacía años.
english: My aunt had been living alone for years.

target: En aquella empresa no pagaban bien.
english: At that company, they didn't pay well.

target: Hacía mucho calor aquel día.
english: It was very hot that day.

target: Todos pensábamos que era broma.
english: We all thought it was a joke.

target: Antes íbamos de vacaciones a la montaña.
english: We used to go on vacation to the mountains.

target: Yo tenía unos diez años cuando nos mudamos.
english: I was about ten when we moved.

target: Ella parecía nerviosa.
english: She seemed nervous.

target: De pequeño odiaba las verduras.
english: As a kid, I hated vegetables.

target: En esa época el alquiler costaba la mitad.
english: At that time, rent cost half as much.

target: Mi profesor favorito siempre usaba corbata.
english: My favorite teacher always wore a tie.

target: Antes había más árboles en esta calle.
english: There used to be more trees on this street.

target: Ella trabajaba mientras estudiaba.
english: She worked while she studied.

target: De joven creía que todo era posible.
english: When I was young, I believed anything was possible.

target: Mis padres se querían mucho.
english: My parents loved each other a lot.

target: La casa olía a café recién hecho.
english: The house smelled like freshly brewed coffee.

target: Antes desayunaba en una cafetería cerca de la oficina.
english: I used to have breakfast at a café near the office.

target: El profesor hablaba muy rápido.
english: The teacher spoke very fast.

target: En aquella época no conocía a nadie aquí.
english: At that time, I didn't know anyone here.

target: Siempre cenábamos juntos en familia.
english: We always used to have dinner together as a family.

target: Me despierto sin alarma.
english: I wake up without an alarm.
grammar: Despertarse — reflexive for self

target: Ella se ducha por la mañana.
english: She showers in the morning.

target: Nos vamos a las ocho.
english: We're leaving at eight.
grammar: Irse — reflexive changes meaning from ir

target: Él se afeita cada dos días.
english: He shaves every other day.

target: Me acuesto tarde los viernes.
english: I go to bed late on Fridays.

target: Se visten muy bien para la oficina.
english: They dress very well for the office.

target: Ella se maquilla en cinco minutos.
english: She does her makeup in five minutes.

target: Nos aburrimos en esa clase.
english: We get bored in that class.

target: Él se queja de todo.
english: He complains about everything.

target: Me siento mal por lo que dije.
english: I feel bad about what I said.

target: Ella se enfada fácilmente.
english: She gets angry easily.

target: Nos reunimos cada lunes.
english: We meet every Monday.
grammar: Reunirse — reciprocal reflexive

target: Él se prepara un café antes de salir.
english: He makes himself a coffee before leaving.

target: Me pongo nervioso antes de hablar en público.
english: I get nervous before speaking in public.

target: Ella se fue sin decir adiós.
english: She left without saying goodbye.

target: Nos sentamos en la terraza.
english: We sat on the terrace.

target: Me olvido de todo últimamente.
english: I forget everything lately.

target: Se llevan muy bien.
english: They get along very well.
grammar: Llevarse bien — reciprocal

target: Me quedo en casa esta noche.
english: I'm staying home tonight.
grammar: Quedarse — reflexive for staying

target: Ella se preocupa mucho por su madre.
english: She worries a lot about her mother.

target: Me divierto mucho con mis amigos.
english: I have a lot of fun with my friends.

target: Él se lesionó jugando al baloncesto.
english: He got injured playing basketball.

target: Nos llamamos por teléfono cada semana.
english: We call each other every week.
grammar: Llamarse — reciprocal

target: Me visto rápido cuando llego tarde.
english: I get dressed quickly when I'm late.

target: Ella se acostumbró al frío.
english: She got used to the cold.

target: Se parecen mucho el padre y el hijo.
english: The father and son look very similar.
grammar: Parecerse — reciprocal

target: Me seco el pelo con secador.
english: I dry my hair with a hair dryer.

target: Él se concentra mejor con música.
english: He concentrates better with music.

target: Me atrevo a probar cosas nuevas.
english: I dare to try new things.

target: Nos encontramos en la cafetería.
english: We met at the café.
grammar: Encontrarse — reciprocal

target: Ella se pintó las uñas de rojo.
english: She painted her nails red.

target: Me caí en la calle y me hice daño.
english: I fell in the street and hurt myself.
grammar: Caerse — reflexive for accidental

target: Él se puso contento al oír la noticia.
english: He became happy when he heard the news.

target: Me quito los zapatos al llegar a casa.
english: I take off my shoes when I get home.

target: Nos escribimos por WhatsApp.
english: We text each other on WhatsApp.
grammar: Escribirse — reciprocal

target: Ella se peina antes de salir.
english: She combs her hair before leaving.

target: Me relajo leyendo un libro.
english: I relax by reading a book.

target: Él se equivocó de puerta.
english: He went to the wrong door.
grammar: Equivocarse — reflexive for making a mistake

target: Nos mudamos juntos el próximo mes.
english: We're moving in together next month.

target: Me lavo las manos antes de comer.
english: I wash my hands before eating.

target: Se despidió con un abrazo.
english: She said goodbye with a hug.

target: Me imagino que ya lo sabes.
english: I imagine you already know.

target: Ella se negó a firmar el contrato.
english: She refused to sign the contract.

target: Me la paso bien aquí.
english: I have a good time here.

target: Ellos se ayudan mutuamente.
english: They help each other.
grammar: Reciprocal reflexive

target: Me acerqué a preguntarle.
english: I approached to ask her.

target: Ella se da cuenta de todo.
english: She notices everything.
grammar: Darse cuenta — reflexive idiom

target: Me alegro mucho por ti.
english: I'm very happy for you.

target: Se me rompieron las gafas.
english: My glasses broke.
grammar: Se me — accidental se construction

target: Ella se inscribió en un curso de cocina.
english: She signed up for a cooking class.

target: Me siento orgulloso de mi equipo.
english: I feel proud of my team.

target: Él se dedica a la fotografía.
english: He works in photography.
grammar: Dedicarse a — reflexive for occupation

target: Nos echamos de menos.
english: We miss each other.
grammar: Echarse de menos — reciprocal

target: Me acordé de ti ayer.
english: I thought of you yesterday.
grammar: Acordarse de — reflexive for remembering

target: Gracias por todo.
english: Thanks for everything.

target: Paso por tu casa a las ocho.
english: I'll swing by your house at eight.
grammar: Por for passing through / by a place

target: Trabajo para una empresa de tecnología.
english: I work for a tech company.
grammar: Para for employer / who you work for

target: Te llamé por teléfono tres veces.
english: I called you by phone three times.
grammar: Por for means of communication

target: Necesito esto para mañana.
english: I need this for tomorrow.
grammar: Para for deadline

target: Vamos a caminar por el parque.
english: Let's walk through the park.
grammar: Por for movement through a place

target: Estudio español para viajar por Latinoamérica.
english: I study Spanish to travel through Latin America.
grammar: Para for purpose; por for through

target: ¿Por qué no viniste a la fiesta?
english: Why didn't you come to the party?

target: Pagué veinte euros por la camiseta.
english: I paid twenty euros for the shirt.
grammar: Por for exchange / price paid

target: Para ser extranjero, hablas muy bien.
english: For a foreigner, you speak very well.
grammar: Para for considering / given that

target: Vengo por la mañana.
english: I come in the morning.
grammar: Por for general time of day

target: Esta carta es para el director.
english: This letter is for the director.
grammar: Para for intended recipient

target: Salgo para Madrid el lunes.
english: I leave for Madrid on Monday.
grammar: Para for destination

target: Por lo menos tenemos el fin de semana.
english: At least we have the weekend.

target: No te preocupes por el dinero.
english: Don't worry about the money.

target: Estoy aquí por una reunión.
english: I'm here for a meeting.
grammar: Por for reason / cause

target: Para llegar a tiempo, salimos temprano.
english: To arrive on time, we left early.
grammar: Para + infinitive for purpose / goal

target: Hablo por experiencia.
english: I speak from experience.

target: Voy por agua.
english: I'm going for water.
grammar: Por for in search of / to get

target: Para el viernes necesito el informe.
english: I need the report by Friday.
grammar: Para for deadline

target: ¿Por cuánto tiempo te quedas?
english: For how long are you staying?
grammar: Por for duration of time

target: Esto es para la cena de esta noche.
english: This is for tonight's dinner.

target: Caminamos por la playa al atardecer.
english: We walked along the beach at sunset.

target: Para mí es muy importante la familia.
english: For me, family is very important.
grammar: Para mí for personal opinion

target: Fui al médico por el dolor de espalda.
english: I went to the doctor because of the back pain.
grammar: Por for cause / reason

target: Para empezar, necesitamos un plan.
english: To start, we need a plan.

target: Te cambio mi turno por el tuyo.
english: I'll trade you my shift for yours.
grammar: Por for exchange / trade

target: Estoy listo para el examen.
english: I'm ready for the exam.

target: Por suerte encontré un taxi.
english: Luckily I found a taxi.

target: Cocino para toda la familia.
english: I cook for the whole family.

target: Viajamos por carretera.
english: We traveled by road.
grammar: Por for means of transport / route

target: Para ser lunes, no está mal.
english: For a Monday, it's not bad.

target: Pregunta por María cuando llegues.
english: Ask for María when you arrive.
grammar: Preguntar por — to ask for / about someone

target: El vuelo es para las cinco de la tarde.
english: The flight is for five in the afternoon.

target: Me quedé en casa por la lluvia.
english: I stayed home because of the rain.
grammar: Por for cause

target: Para variar, hoy no hay tráfico.
english: For a change, there's no traffic today.

target: Todavía me quedan cosas por hacer.
english: I still have things left to do.

target: Este parque es perfecto para los niños.
english: This park is perfect for kids.

target: Por fin llegó el verano.
english: Summer has finally arrived.

target: Ahorro dinero para las vacaciones.
english: I save money for vacation.

target: No puedo ir por el trabajo.
english: I can't go because of work.
grammar: Por for cause / obstacle

target: Para serte sincero, no me gustó.
english: To be honest with you, I didn't like it.

target: Manejo por esta calle todos los días.
english: I drive down this street every day.

target: Para colmo, se me olvidaron las llaves.
english: To top it off, I forgot my keys.

target: Brindamos por los novios.
english: We toasted to the couple.
grammar: Por for in honor of

target: Para cuando llegues, ya estará listo.
english: By the time you arrive, it'll be ready.

target: Multiplicamos cinco por tres.
english: We multiply five by three.
grammar: Por for multiplication

target: Para el cumpleaños de mi madre, vamos a un restaurante.
english: For my mother's birthday, we're going to a restaurant.

target: Paseamos por el centro de la ciudad.
english: We strolled through the city center.

target: Por lo general, como en casa.
english: Generally, I eat at home.

target: Se lo dije ayer.
english: I told him yesterday.
grammar: Se lo — indirect + direct pronoun combo

target: ¿Me puedes ayudar un momento?
english: Can you help me for a moment?

target: Te lo explico ahora.
english: I'll explain it to you now.
grammar: Te (indirect) + lo (direct) — double pronoun

target: ¿Les mandaste el correo?
english: Did you send them the email?
grammar: Les — indirect object pronoun, plural

target: Lo veo todos los días en el trabajo.
english: I see him every day at work.

target: Nos invitaron a la boda.
english: They invited us to the wedding.

target: Le pregunté pero no me contestó.
english: I asked him but he didn't answer me.

target: ¿La puedo llamar mañana?
english: Can I call her tomorrow?

target: No me lo puedo creer.
english: I can't believe it.
grammar: Me lo — double pronoun with modal verb

target: Se lo prometí a mi hijo.
english: I promised it to my son.
grammar: Se replaces le before lo/la

target: Los vi en el supermercado.
english: I saw them at the supermarket.

target: Le traje un café.
english: I brought him a coffee.

target: No nos avisaron del cambio.
english: They didn't tell us about the change.
grammar: Nos — indirect object, first person plural

target: ¿Me lo puedes repetir?
english: Can you repeat that for me?

target: Se las envié esta mañana.
english: I sent them to her this morning.
grammar: Se las — se replaces le; las is feminine plural direct object

target: La conozco desde hace años.
english: I've known her for years.

target: Le di las gracias al profesor.
english: I thanked the teacher.

target: ¿Nos puedes llevar al aeropuerto?
english: Can you take us to the airport?

target: Lo siento, no lo sabía.
english: I'm sorry, I didn't know.

target: Les pedí que vinieran temprano.
english: I asked them to come early.

target: Te lo mando por correo.
english: I'll send it to you by email.

target: Se lo conté a mi hermana.
english: I told my sister about it.

target: No la toques, está caliente.
english: Don't touch it, it's hot.

target: Le compré un regalo a mi madre.
english: I bought my mother a gift.
grammar: Le — indirect object; a mi madre clarifies recipient

target: ¿Me dejas tu bolígrafo?
english: Can you lend me your pen?

target: Las puse en la mesa.
english: I put them on the table.

target: Nos lo pasamos muy bien.
english: We had a great time.

target: Le dije que no se preocupara.
english: I told him not to worry.

target: No lo he probado nunca.
english: I've never tried it.

target: ¿Te lo has pensado bien?
english: Have you thought it through?

target: Me la presentó en una fiesta.
english: He introduced her to me at a party.
grammar: Me (indirect) + la (direct) — double pronoun

target: Los llamé pero no contestaron.
english: I called them but they didn't answer.

target: Le hace falta descansar.
english: He needs to rest.
grammar: Le + hacer falta — indirect object with impersonal verb

target: Ponlo aquí, por favor.
english: Put it here, please.
grammar: Lo attached to affirmative tú command

target: Se lo dejé en su escritorio.
english: I left it for him on his desk.

target: No me importa esperar.
english: I don't mind waiting.

target: Le preocupa el examen.
english: The exam worries him.
grammar: Le — indirect object with preocupar (gustar-type)

target: Te las traigo mañana.
english: I'll bring them to you tomorrow.

target: Me lo dijeron en la oficina.
english: They told me at the office.

target: No nos lo esperábamos.
english: We weren't expecting it.

target: Le cuesta levantarse temprano.
english: He has a hard time getting up early.
grammar: Le + costar — indirect object with costar

target: Se lo regalé por su cumpleaños.
english: I gave it to her for her birthday.

target: ¿La ves? Está ahí al fondo.
english: Do you see her? She's over there at the back.

target: Le pedí el día libre al jefe.
english: I asked the boss for the day off.

target: Se lo compré en el mercado.
english: I bought it for her at the market.

target: ¿Nos lo confirmas antes del viernes?
english: Can you confirm it for us before Friday?

target: Los recogí del colegio.
english: I picked them up from school.

target: Me faltan dos páginas.
english: I'm missing two pages.
grammar: Me + faltar — indirect object with faltar

target: Le presté mi carro a mi hermano.
english: I lent my car to my brother.

target: No te lo tomes a mal.
english: Don't take it the wrong way.

target: Espero que llegues a tiempo.
english: I hope you arrive on time.
grammar: Espero que + subjunctive — wish/hope trigger

target: Quiero que sepas la verdad.
english: I want you to know the truth.

target: Es importante que hables con él.
english: It's important that you talk to him.
grammar: Es importante que + subjunctive — impersonal trigger

target: Dudo que tenga tiempo esta semana.
english: I doubt he has time this week.

target: Ojalá haga buen tiempo mañana.
english: I hope the weather is good tomorrow.
grammar: Ojalá + subjunctive — wish/hope

target: No creo que sea buena idea.
english: I don't think it's a good idea.

target: Mi madre quiere que la llame más.
english: My mother wants me to call her more.
grammar: Querer que + subjunctive — will/desire trigger

target: Es necesario que terminemos hoy.
english: It's necessary that we finish today.

target: Espero que te mejores pronto.
english: I hope you feel better soon.

target: No es verdad que cueste tanto.
english: It's not true that it costs that much.
grammar: Negated truth claim + subjunctive

target: Necesito que me envíes el archivo.
english: I need you to send me the file.
grammar: Necesitar que + subjunctive

target: Antes de que te vayas, firma esto.
english: Before you leave, sign this.
grammar: Antes de que always takes subjunctive

target: Me alegra que estés aquí.
english: I'm glad you're here.

target: Es raro que no conteste el teléfono.
english: It's strange that he's not answering the phone.

target: Prefiero que me lo digas directamente.
english: I prefer that you tell me directly.

target: Cuando llegues a casa, llámame.
english: When you get home, call me.
grammar: Cuando + subjunctive for future actions

target: Quiero que vengas a la cena.
english: I want you to come to the dinner.

target: Es posible que llueva esta tarde.
english: It's possible it'll rain this afternoon.
grammar: Es posible que + subjunctive — uncertainty

target: No creo que encuentre trabajo tan rápido.
english: I don't think he'll find a job that fast.

target: Ojalá pueda ir a tu boda.
english: I hope I can go to your wedding.

target: Es mejor que salgamos temprano.
english: It's better that we leave early.

target: Siento que tengas que pasar por esto.
english: I'm sorry you have to go through this.

target: Dile que me llame cuando pueda.
english: Tell him to call me when he can.

target: No quiero que te preocupes por nada.
english: I don't want you to worry about anything.

target: Busco un hotel que tenga piscina.
english: I'm looking for a hotel that has a pool.
grammar: Subjunctive in adjective clause — unknown/nonexistent antecedent

target: Necesito que alguien me ayude con esto.
english: I need someone to help me with this.

target: Espero que les guste la comida.
english: I hope they like the food.

target: Quiero que todo salga bien.
english: I want everything to go well.

target: A menos que llueva, vamos a la playa.
english: Unless it rains, we're going to the beach.
grammar: A menos que always takes subjunctive

target: Me pide que llegue antes de las nueve.
english: He asks me to arrive before nine.
grammar: Pedir que + subjunctive — request

target: Es probable que cambiemos de oficina.
english: It's likely we'll change offices.

target: Ojalá tenga un buen día.
english: I hope you have a good day.

target: Me sorprende que no lo sepas.
english: I'm surprised you don't know.
grammar: Emotion trigger + subjunctive

target: Aunque llueva, voy a salir.
english: Even if it rains, I'm going out.
grammar: Aunque + subjunctive for hypothetical concession

target: Quiero que mis hijos sean felices.
english: I want my children to be happy.

target: Hace falta que hablemos del presupuesto.
english: We need to talk about the budget.

target: No es seguro que venga mañana.
english: It's not certain he'll come tomorrow.

target: Deja que te ayude con las maletas.
english: Let me help you with the suitcases.
grammar: Dejar que + subjunctive — allowing

target: Espero que disfrutes del viaje.
english: I hope you enjoy the trip.

target: Si no trabajas aquí, no lo entiendes.
english: If you don't work here, you don't understand.

target: Me encanta que seas tan honesto.
english: I love that you're so honest.

target: Que yo sepa, la tienda cierra a las ocho.
english: As far as I know, the store closes at eight.

target: No dejes que te molesten.
english: Don't let them bother you.

target: Es fundamental que lleguemos puntuales.
english: It's essential that we arrive on time.
grammar: Es fundamental que + subjunctive

target: Para que entiendas, te lo voy a explicar.
english: So that you understand, I'll explain it to you.
grammar: Para que always takes subjunctive

target: Ojalá encontremos un buen restaurante.
english: I hope we find a good restaurant.

target: No parece que vaya a mejorar.
english: It doesn't seem like it's going to improve.

target: Quiero que descanses este fin de semana.
english: I want you to rest this weekend.

target: Puede que tengamos que cancelar la reunión.
english: We might have to cancel the meeting.
grammar: Puede que + subjunctive — possibility

target: Es hora de que busques otro empleo.
english: It's time you looked for another job.

target: Lamento que no hayas podido venir.
english: I'm sorry you weren't able to come.

target: Sin que nadie se dé cuenta, salimos.
english: Without anyone noticing, we left.
grammar: Sin que + subjunctive

target: Recomiendo que pruebes la paella.
english: I recommend you try the paella.

target: No te preocupes, todo va a salir bien.
english: Don't worry, everything will be fine.

target: Dime qué necesitas.
english: Tell me what you need.
grammar: Tú affirmative command: di + me

target: Ven aquí un momento.
english: Come here for a moment.

target: No toques eso, por favor.
english: Don't touch that, please.

target: Siéntese, por favor.
english: Please, have a seat.
grammar: Usted command: siéntese — formal

target: Apaga la luz cuando salgas.
english: Turn off the light when you leave.

target: Escucha, tengo que decirte algo.
english: Listen, I have to tell you something.

target: No le digas nada todavía.
english: Don't tell him anything yet.
grammar: Negative tú command uses subjunctive: no digas

target: Pásame la sal, por favor.
english: Pass me the salt, please.

target: Sal de ahí ahora mismo.
english: Get out of there right now.

target: No te olvides de llamar al dentista.
english: Don't forget to call the dentist.

target: Envíeme el presupuesto por correo.
english: Send me the budget by email.
grammar: Usted command: envíe + me

target: Pon la mesa, que ya está la cena.
english: Set the table, dinner's ready.

target: No corras por el pasillo.
english: Don't run in the hallway.

target: Haz la tarea antes de jugar.
english: Do your homework before playing.
grammar: Irregular tú command: haz (hacer)

target: Ten cuidado con el escalón.
english: Be careful with the step.
grammar: Irregular tú command: ten (tener)

target: No comas tan rápido.
english: Don't eat so fast.

target: Abre la ventana, hace mucho calor.
english: Open the window, it's very hot.

target: Trae el paraguas por si acaso.
english: Bring the umbrella just in case.

target: No te vayas todavía.
english: Don't go yet.

target: Sé paciente, ya casi estamos.
english: Be patient, we're almost there.
grammar: Irregular tú command: sé (ser)

target: Cierra la puerta con llave.
english: Lock the door.

target: No hables tan alto en la biblioteca.
english: Don't talk so loud in the library.

target: Léete las instrucciones primero.
english: Read the instructions first.

target: Ponte el abrigo, que hace frío.
english: Put your coat on, it's cold.
grammar: Reflexive tú command: ponte (ponerse)

target: No dejes todo para el último momento.
english: Don't leave everything for the last minute.

target: Siga todo recto y gire a la derecha.
english: Go straight ahead and turn right.
grammar: Usted commands: siga, gire — for directions

target: Espera un momento, ahora vuelvo.
english: Wait a moment, I'll be right back.

target: Cuéntame qué pasó.
english: Tell me what happened.

target: No te sientes ahí, está mojado.
english: Don't sit there, it's wet.

target: Prueba este plato, está delicioso.
english: Try this dish, it's delicious.

target: Avísame cuando termines.
english: Let me know when you finish.

target: No gastes tanto dinero en tonterías.
english: Don't spend so much money on nonsense.

target: Busca en el cajón de abajo.
english: Look in the bottom drawer.

target: Acuéstate temprano, mañana hay que madrugar.
english: Go to bed early, we have to get up early tomorrow.

target: Pregúntale al recepcionista.
english: Ask the receptionist.

target: No llegues tarde otra vez.
english: Don't be late again.

target: Toma lo que necesites.
english: Take whatever you need.

target: Cállate un segundo, estoy hablando.
english: Be quiet for a second, I'm talking.
grammar: Reflexive tú command: cállate (callarse)

target: Mira, ahí está el museo.
english: Look, there's the museum.

target: No te rindas, lo estás haciendo bien.
english: Don't give up, you're doing great.

target: Firma aquí abajo, por favor.
english: Sign here at the bottom, please.

target: Recoge tus cosas del suelo.
english: Pick your things up off the floor.

target: No me interrumpas, por favor.
english: Don't interrupt me, please.

target: Deja de quejarte y haz algo.
english: Stop complaining and do something.

target: Descansa, te lo mereces.
english: Rest, you deserve it.

target: Ve al supermercado y compra leche.
english: Go to the supermarket and buy milk.
grammar: Irregular tú command: ve (ir)

target: Perdone, ¿me puede indicar el camino?
english: Excuse me, can you show me the way?

target: No hagas ruido, el bebé está durmiendo.
english: Don't make noise, the baby is sleeping.

target: Respira hondo y tranquilízate.
english: Take a deep breath and calm down.

target: Sígueme, te enseño la oficina.
english: Follow me, I'll show you the office.

target: No abras la puerta a desconocidos.
english: Don't open the door to strangers.

target: Déjame pensarlo un poco.
english: Let me think about it.

target: Sube el volumen, no oigo nada.
english: Turn up the volume, I can't hear anything.

target: Yo en tu lugar buscaría otro trabajo.
english: In your place, I would look for another job.
grammar: Conditional for advice — yo en tu lugar + conditional

target: Me gustaría viajar a Japón.
english: I would like to travel to Japan.
grammar: Me gustaría — polite wish with conditional

target: ¿Podrías cerrar la ventana?
english: Could you close the window?
grammar: Podrías — polite request with conditional

target: Sería mejor empezar temprano.
english: It would be better to start early.

target: No haría eso si fuera tú.
english: I wouldn't do that if I were you.

target: ¿Te importaría bajar la música?
english: Would you mind turning down the music?
grammar: Conditional for polite request

target: Deberías ir al médico.
english: You should go to the doctor.

target: Con más práctica, hablarías mejor.
english: With more practice, you would speak better.

target: Me encantaría conocer a tu familia.
english: I would love to meet your family.

target: ¿Podrías enviarme el documento antes del lunes?
english: Could you send me the document before Monday?

target: Yo no diría nada por ahora.
english: I wouldn't say anything for now.

target: Si pudiera elegir, viviría cerca del mar.
english: If I could choose, I would live near the sea.

target: Tendría que pensármelo.
english: I would have to think about it.

target: ¿Querrías venir con nosotros?
english: Would you like to come with us?

target: Si lloviera, nos quedaríamos en casa.
english: If it rained, we would stay home.

target: Deberíamos salir ya para no llegar tarde.
english: We should leave now so we're not late.

target: Con un poco de suerte, conseguiríamos el contrato.
english: With a bit of luck, we would get the contract.

target: ¿Podrías repetir eso más despacio?
english: Could you repeat that more slowly?

target: Si no trabajara tanto, pasaría más tiempo con mis hijos.
english: If I didn't work so much, I would spend more time with my kids.

target: Sería genial ir todos juntos.
english: It would be great to all go together.

target: Yo diría que tiene razón.
english: I would say she's right.

target: Si viviera más cerca, vendría a pie.
english: If I lived closer, I would come on foot.

target: ¿Te gustaría cenar fuera esta noche?
english: Would you like to eat out tonight?

target: No debería comer tanto dulce.
english: I shouldn't eat so many sweets.

target: Con más personal, terminaríamos antes.
english: With more staff, we would finish sooner.
grammar: Conditional for hypothetical result

target: Si fuera posible, cambiaría mi horario.
english: If it were possible, I would change my schedule.

target: ¿Podrías ayudarme con la mudanza?
english: Could you help me with the move?

target: Creo que sería mejor esperar.
english: I think it would be better to wait.

target: ¿Sabrías llegar sin GPS?
english: Would you know how to get there without GPS?

target: Si hablara mejor español, pediría un traslado.
english: If I spoke better Spanish, I would request a transfer.

target: Nos gustaría reservar una mesa para cuatro.
english: We would like to reserve a table for four.
grammar: Nos gustaría — polite conditional request

target: Si estuviera en tu situación, hablaría con ella.
english: If I were in your situation, I would talk to her.

target: Eso no pasaría en una empresa seria.
english: That wouldn't happen at a serious company.

target: A mí me parecería bien cambiar la fecha.
english: I would be fine with changing the date.

target: Si pudiera volver atrás, haría las cosas de otra manera.
english: If I could go back, I would do things differently.

target: Deberías probar la comida de aquí.
english: You should try the food here.

target: Si no lloviera, iríamos al parque.
english: If it weren't raining, we would go to the park.

target: ¿Le importaría esperar un momento?
english: Would you mind waiting a moment?
grammar: Conditional of importar — very formal polite request

target: Yo que tú no aceptaría esa oferta.
english: If I were you, I wouldn't accept that offer.
grammar: Yo que tú + conditional — giving advice

target: Nos encantaría que vinieras a la boda.
english: We would love for you to come to the wedding.

target: Con este tráfico, tardaríamos una hora.
english: With this traffic, it would take us an hour.

target: Si me subieran el sueldo, estaría más contento.
english: If they gave me a raise, I would be happier.

target: Diría que tiene unos treinta años.
english: I would say he's about thirty years old.

target: Si no costara tanto, iría todos los años.
english: If it didn't cost so much, I would go every year.

target: ¿Podrías echarle un ojo a esto?
english: Could you take a look at this?

target: Me mudaría a otra ciudad sin pensarlo.
english: I would move to another city without thinking twice.

target: Ya habrán llegado a esta hora.
english: They must have arrived by now.
grammar: Future perfect for probability about the past

target: Mañana te llamaré por la tarde.
english: I'll call you tomorrow afternoon.

target: ¿Habrás terminado para las cinco?
english: Will you have finished by five?
grammar: Future perfect for completed action before a deadline

target: Creo que lloverá este fin de semana.
english: I think it'll rain this weekend.

target: He vivido aquí toda mi vida.
english: I've lived here my whole life.
grammar: Present perfect — life experience

target: Nunca he estado en Sudamérica.
english: I've never been to South America.

target: ¿Quién será a esta hora?
english: Who could that be at this hour?
grammar: Future tense for speculation / probability in present

target: Ya habíamos comido cuando llegaron.
english: We had already eaten when they arrived.
grammar: Past perfect — action completed before another past action

target: El próximo año estudiaré un máster.
english: Next year I'll study for a master's degree.

target: ¿Has visto las noticias hoy?
english: Have you seen the news today?

target: Todavía no he desayunado.
english: I still haven't had breakfast.

target: Serán las tres de la tarde.
english: It must be about three in the afternoon.
grammar: Future for conjecture about present time

target: Cuando vuelva, ya habrán cerrado.
english: When I get back, they'll have already closed.

target: He trabajado aquí cinco años.
english: I've worked here for five years.

target: El mes que viene nos mudaremos.
english: Next month we'll move.

target: Nunca había visto algo así.
english: I had never seen anything like that.
grammar: Past perfect — first-time experience in the past

target: ¿Cuánto costará el billete?
english: How much will the ticket cost?

target: Ya he hecho las maletas.
english: I've already packed my suitcases.

target: Habré terminado el informe para el viernes.
english: I'll have finished the report by Friday.
grammar: Future perfect — completion before a deadline

target: ¿Has probado la nueva cafetería?
english: Have you tried the new café?

target: Estará en una reunión, por eso no contesta.
english: He's probably in a meeting, that's why he's not answering.
grammar: Future for probability / speculation about present

target: Nunca habíamos viajado juntos.
english: We had never traveled together.

target: Te prometo que no volverá a pasar.
english: I promise it won't happen again.

target: He pensado mucho en lo que dijiste.
english: I've thought a lot about what you said.

target: Dentro de un año habremos pagado todo.
english: In a year we'll have paid for everything.

target: ¿Cuándo volverás de tu viaje?
english: When will you come back from your trip?

target: Ya había salido cuando me llamaste.
english: I had already left when you called me.
grammar: Past perfect — completed action before past reference

target: Este verano iré a visitar a mis abuelos.
english: This summer I'll go visit my grandparents.

target: ¿Has hablado con el jefe sobre el proyecto?
english: Have you talked to the boss about the project?

target: Supongo que tendrá unos cuarenta años.
english: I guess he's about forty years old.
grammar: Future for conjecture about someone's age

target: Hemos recibido muchas quejas esta semana.
english: We've received a lot of complaints this week.

target: Pronto sabremos los resultados.
english: We'll know the results soon.

target: Ya he leído ese libro dos veces.
english: I've already read that book twice.

target: Para entonces ya habré encontrado trabajo.
english: By then I'll have found a job.
grammar: Future perfect — anticipated completion

target: Nos habíamos perdido en el centro.
english: We had gotten lost in the city center.

target: ¿Vendrás a la cena del viernes?
english: Will you come to Friday's dinner?

target: Le he dicho mil veces que tenga cuidado.
english: I've told him a thousand times to be careful.

target: ¿Habrá sitio en el restaurante?
english: Will there be room at the restaurant?
grammar: Future of haber for speculation

target: Todavía no hemos decidido adónde ir.
english: We still haven't decided where to go.

target: El año que viene cambiaré de trabajo.
english: Next year I'll change jobs.

target: Ya habían cerrado cuando llegamos.
english: They had already closed when we arrived.
grammar: Past perfect — action completed before arrival

target: Seguro que ya lo habrás oído.
english: I'm sure you'll have heard about it already.
grammar: Future perfect for assumption

target: He aprendido mucho en este trabajo.
english: I've learned a lot at this job.

target: ¿A qué hora saldrá el tren?
english: What time will the train leave?

target: Nos hemos mudado tres veces este año.
english: We've moved three times this year.

target: Ya habíamos hablado de esto antes.
english: We had already talked about this before.

target: No creo que tarde mucho.
english: I don't think I'll take long.

target: Se habrá olvidado de la reunión.
english: He must have forgotten about the meeting.
grammar: Future perfect for probability about recent past

target: He conocido a gente muy interesante aquí.
english: I've met very interesting people here.

target: ¿Alguna vez has cocinado paella?
english: Have you ever cooked paella?

target: Para junio ya habremos acabado las obras.
english: By June we'll have finished the construction.
grammar: Future perfect for projected completion

target: Nunca había probado la comida tailandesa.
english: I had never tried Thai food.

target: ¿Me ayudarás con la mudanza el sábado?
english: Will you help me with the move on Saturday?

target: He intentado llamarte varias veces.
english: I've tried calling you several times.

target: No sé qué habrá pasado.
english: I don't know what could have happened.
grammar: Future perfect for speculation about unknown past event

target: La chica que trabaja aquí es mi prima.
english: The girl who works here is my cousin.
grammar: Que as relative pronoun referring to people

target: El libro que me prestaste es muy bueno.
english: The book you lent me is very good.

target: La tienda donde compro la fruta está cerrada.
english: The store where I buy fruit is closed.

target: El hombre que vive al lado es muy amable.
english: The man who lives next door is very kind.

target: Lo que me dijiste ayer me sorprendió.
english: What you told me yesterday surprised me.
grammar: Lo que — what/that which, for abstract ideas

target: La empresa donde trabajo tiene oficinas en Madrid.
english: The company where I work has offices in Madrid.

target: El restaurante que te recomendé está en esa calle.
english: The restaurant I recommended to you is on that street.

target: La persona con quien hablé me dio buena información.
english: The person I spoke with gave me good information.
grammar: Con quien — with whom, used after prepositions

target: Los estudiantes que aprobaron el examen están contentos.
english: The students who passed the exam are happy.

target: La ciudad donde crecí ha cambiado mucho.
english: The city where I grew up has changed a lot.

target: Lo que necesitas es descansar un poco.
english: What you need is to rest a little.

target: El profesor que nos enseña historia es muy divertido.
english: The teacher who teaches us history is very fun.

target: La casa que compraron mis padres tiene jardín.
english: The house my parents bought has a garden.

target: El médico que me atendió fue muy profesional.
english: The doctor who treated me was very professional.

target: La película que vimos anoche me encantó.
english: I loved the movie we watched last night.

target: El cliente con quien tengo la reunión llega a las tres.
english: The client I have the meeting with arrives at three.
grammar: Con quien — preposition + quien for people

target: La calle donde aparqué el carro está lejos.
english: The street where I parked the car is far away.

target: Lo que más me gusta de mi trabajo es el equipo.
english: What I like most about my job is the team.

target: La vecina que tiene gatos siempre es muy simpática.
english: The neighbor who has cats is always very nice.

target: El hotel donde nos quedamos tenía una piscina enorme.
english: The hotel where we stayed had a huge pool.

target: La razón por la que llegué tarde fue el tráfico.
english: The reason I arrived late was the traffic.
grammar: Por la que — for which, compound relative

target: El compañero que me ayudó es nuevo en la oficina.
english: The coworker who helped me is new in the office.

target: La mujer cuyo hijo estudia conmigo es abogada.
english: The woman whose son studies with me is a lawyer.
grammar: Cuyo — whose, agrees with the possessed noun

target: El parque donde corremos está muy bien cuidado.
english: The park where we run is very well maintained.

target: Lo que dijo el jefe no tenía sentido.
english: What the boss said didn't make sense.

target: El proyecto en el que estoy trabajando es complicado.
english: The project I'm working on is complicated.
grammar: En el que — in which, preposition + article + que

target: La canción que está sonando me recuerda a mi infancia.
english: The song that's playing reminds me of my childhood.

target: El país donde nací tiene playas hermosas.
english: The country where I was born has beautiful beaches.

target: Las personas que conocí en el viaje eran muy agradables.
english: The people I met on the trip were very nice.

target: Lo que me preocupa es que no tenemos suficiente tiempo.
english: What worries me is that we don't have enough time.

target: El barrio donde vivo es muy tranquilo.
english: The neighborhood where I live is very quiet.

target: La amiga que me presentaste es muy graciosa.
english: The friend you introduced me to is very funny.

target: El informe que escribí ayer tiene un error.
english: The report I wrote yesterday has an error.

target: La playa donde fuimos el verano pasado estaba limpia.
english: The beach where we went last summer was clean.

target: El tema del que hablamos ayer es importante.
english: The topic we talked about yesterday is important.
grammar: Del que — about which, contraction de + el + que

target: La mesa que reservé es para cuatro personas.
english: The table I reserved is for four people.

target: El chico con quien sale mi hermana es muy simpático.
english: The guy my sister is dating is very nice.

target: Lo que pasó ayer no puede volver a pasar.
english: What happened yesterday cannot happen again.

target: La familia que vive arriba tiene dos niños pequeños.
english: The family that lives upstairs has two small children.

target: El dentista que me recomendaste es muy bueno.
english: The dentist you recommended to me is very good.

target: La forma en que habla me recuerda a mi abuelo.
english: The way he speaks reminds me of my grandfather.
grammar: En que — in which, relative with preposition

target: El autobús que tomo sale a las siete y media.
english: The bus I take leaves at seven thirty.

target: Lo que más echo de menos es la comida de mi madre.
english: What I miss the most is my mother's cooking.

target: El lugar donde cenamos anoche era bastante caro.
english: The place where we had dinner last night was quite expensive.

target: Los colegas con quienes almuerzo son de otro departamento.
english: The colleagues I have lunch with are from another department.
grammar: Con quienes — with whom, plural

target: La carta que recibí del banco me pone nervioso.
english: The letter I received from the bank makes me nervous.

target: El pueblo donde veranea mi familia es precioso.
english: The town where my family spends summers is lovely.

target: Lo que no entiendo es por qué no me llamaron.
english: What I don't understand is why they didn't call me.

target: La receta que encontré en internet salió muy bien.
english: The recipe I found online turned out very well.

target: El día en que nos conocimos llovía mucho.
english: The day we met it was raining a lot.

target: La oficina donde trabajaba antes era más pequeña.
english: The office where I used to work was smaller.

target: El problema que tenemos es la falta de presupuesto.
english: The problem we have is the lack of budget.

target: La persona a quien llamé no me contestó.
english: The person I called didn't answer me.
grammar: A quien — whom, direct object with preposition a

target: El regalo que le compré a mi madre le gustó mucho.
english: My mother really liked the gift I bought her.

target: Me gustaría que vinieras a mi fiesta de cumpleaños.
english: I'd like you to come to my birthday party.

target: El jefe quería que termináramos el informe antes del viernes.
english: The boss wanted us to finish the report before Friday.
grammar: Quería que + imperfect subjunctive — past wish/demand

target: Si ganara la lotería, dejaría mi trabajo.
english: If I won the lottery, I would quit my job.

target: Ojalá tuviera más vacaciones.
english: I wish I had more vacation time.
grammar: Ojalá + imperfect subjunctive — wish unlikely to come true

target: Si hablara mejor inglés, buscaría trabajo en el extranjero.
english: If I spoke better English, I would look for work abroad.

target: Me pidió que le ayudara con la mudanza.
english: He asked me to help him with the move.

target: Quería que supieran la verdad antes de tomar una decisión.
english: I wanted them to know the truth before making a decision.

target: Si fuera más joven, viajaría por todo el mundo.
english: If I were younger, I would travel around the world.

target: Mi madre me dijo que no saliera tan tarde.
english: My mother told me not to go out so late.

target: Si supiera cocinar mejor, invitaría a mis amigos a cenar.
english: If I knew how to cook better, I would invite my friends over for dinner.

target: Les pedimos que llegaran puntuales a la reunión.
english: We asked them to arrive on time to the meeting.

target: Si tuviera carro, no tardaría tanto en llegar.
english: If I had a car, I wouldn't take so long to get there.

target: Ojalá pudiera quedarme más tiempo.
english: I wish I could stay longer.

target: Si viviera más cerca, iría andando al trabajo.
english: If I lived closer, I would walk to work.

target: El profesor nos recomendó que leyéramos ese libro.
english: The teacher recommended that we read that book.

target: Si no tuviera que trabajar mañana, me quedaría despierto.
english: If I didn't have to work tomorrow, I would stay up.

target: Me sorprendió que nadie dijera nada.
english: It surprised me that nobody said anything.
grammar: Emotion + que + imperfect subjunctive in past

target: Si hiciera buen tiempo, haríamos una barbacoa.
english: If the weather were nice, we would have a barbecue.

target: Necesitábamos que alguien nos tradujera el documento.
english: We needed someone to translate the document for us.

target: Si fuera tú, aceptaría esa oferta de trabajo.
english: If I were you, I would accept that job offer.
grammar: Si fuera tú — if I were you, common advice pattern

target: Dudaba que el paquete llegara a tiempo.
english: I doubted the package would arrive on time.

target: Si pudiera cambiar algo de mi vida, estudiaría otra carrera.
english: If I could change something about my life, I would study a different degree.

target: Mi abuela quería que la visitáramos más a menudo.
english: My grandmother wanted us to visit her more often.

target: Si no costara tanto, compraría un departamento en el centro.
english: If it didn't cost so much, I would buy an apartment downtown.

target: Era importante que todos estuvieran de acuerdo.
english: It was important that everyone agreed.
grammar: Impersonal expression + que + imperfect subjunctive

target: Si hubiera tenido tu número, te habría llamado.
english: If I had had your number, I would have called you.

target: No creía que fuera tan difícil encontrar estacionamiento.
english: I didn't think it would be so hard to find parking.

target: Quería que mi hijo estudiara en una buena universidad.
english: I wanted my son to study at a good university.

target: Si me pagaran más, no buscaría otro empleo.
english: If they paid me more, I wouldn't look for another job.

target: Le sugerí que hablara con su jefe sobre el problema.
english: I suggested he talk to his boss about the problem.

target: Si tuviera jardín, plantaría un huerto.
english: If I had a garden, I would plant a vegetable patch.

target: No pensaba que fuera posible terminar a tiempo.
english: I didn't think it was possible to finish on time.

target: Era necesario que presentáramos los resultados el lunes.
english: It was necessary for us to present the results on Monday.

target: Si no existiera internet, la vida sería muy distinta.
english: If the internet didn't exist, life would be very different.

target: Mi hermano me pidió que le prestara dinero.
english: My brother asked me to lend him money.

target: Si tuviera talento para la música, tocaría el piano.
english: If I had a talent for music, I would play the piano.

target: Esperaba que me llamaran para la entrevista.
english: I was hoping they would call me for the interview.
grammar: Esperaba que + subjunctive — hoping for uncertain outcome

target: Si conociera a alguien en esa ciudad, me mudaría allí.
english: If I knew someone in that city, I would move there.

target: Me alegré de que encontrara trabajo tan rápido.
english: I was glad he found a job so quickly.

target: Si no hiciera tanto frío, saldríamos a pasear.
english: If it weren't so cold, we would go for a walk.
